_autoMaintenance.description: MACHINE-READABLE AUTO-MAINTENANCE RULES — DO NOT REMOVE

rules.trigger: Whenever an upstream commit is cherry-picked or merged into the fork, ALL three .upstream/ tracking files MUST be updated automatically. No user instruction is required.
onSuccessfulCherryPick: 1. Find the commit hash in reviewedCommits[].status
2. If status is SKIPPED → change to IMPLEMENTED
3. If status is IGNORED → DO NOT CHANGE (this is a permanent rejection)
4. Update lastSyncCommit to the newly cherry-picked commit hash
5. Update lastSyncAt to current ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
6. Update lastSyncMessage to the commit title
7. In UPSTREAM_DECISIONS.md: change Status line from SKIPPED to IMPLEMENTED, add 'Imported:' date
8. In IMPLEMENTATION_STATE.md: move commit from 'Awaiting Import' to 'Implemented', update summary counts
onFailedCherryPick: 1. Keep the commit's status as SKIPPED — do not change
2. Log the failure reason in UPSTREAM_DECISIONS.md as a note

crossFileConsistency.rule: STATE.json reviewedCommits[] MUST be a superset of UPSTREAM_DECISIONS.md entries. Every hash in UPSTREAM_DECISIONS.md MUST appear in reviewedCommits[] with matching status. IMPLEMENTATION_STATE.md summaries MUST reflect the same counts.
crossFileConsistency.validation: Before committing any .upstream/ changes, verify: (a) reviewedCommits count matches UPSTREAM_DECISIONS.md entry count, (b) every status matches between files, (c) lastSyncCommit equals the most recently IMPLEMENTED commit (or original baseline if none implemented)

reviewWindowAutoAdvance.rule: When new upstream commits are fetched, lastFetchedCommit updates to the new upstream HEAD. The review window advances from old lastFetchedCommit to new upstream/main HEAD. Already-reviewed commits (those in reviewedCommits[] or UPSTREAM_DECISIONS.md) are automatically filtered out.

lastSyncMessage: test(mcp): fix McpHub Windows command wrapping test ordering (#632)
